title: The Great Wall: A Millennia-Long Project of Unity and Defense
title_zh: 长城：千年防御与统一的工程
intro_zh: 本文约1000词，介绍长城的建造历史、功能演变及其文化象征意义，适合CET-4水平阅读。

paragraphs.en: The Great Wall of China is one of the most iconic structures in the world. Stretching over 21,000 kilometers, it was not built in a single period but over many centuries. The earliest walls date back to the 7th century BC, when various kingdoms erected separate barriers to protect their territories.
paragraphs.zh: 中国的长城是世界上最具有标志性的建筑之一。它绵延超过21000公里，并非在一个时期建成，而是历经多个世纪。最早的城墙可追溯到公元前7世纪，当时各诸侯国修筑了独立的屏障来保护自己的领土。

paragraphs.en: The first unification of these walls occurred under Qin Shi Huang, the first emperor of China, around 220 BC. He ordered the connectivity of existing walls to form a continuous defense line against northern invaders. This massive project required enormous labor, with millions of workers, including soldiers, peasants, and prisoners.
paragraphs.zh: 这些城墙的第一次统一发生在公元前220年左右，中国第一位皇帝秦始皇下令将已有的城墙连接起来，形成一道连续的防线以抵御北方入侵者。这一庞大的工程需要巨大的劳动力，数百万工人参与其中，包括士兵、农民和囚犯。

paragraphs.en: Contrary to popular belief, the Wall was built using various materials. In early periods, builders used rammed earth, stones, and wood. Later, during the Ming Dynasty (1368–1644), bricks and mortar became common. The Ming sections, which are the best preserved today, used a special lime mortar that gave the wall extra strength.
paragraphs.zh: 与普遍看法相反，长城使用了多种材料建造。早期，建筑者使用夯土、石块和木材。后来，在明朝（1368–1644年），砖块和灰浆变得普遍。今天保存最完好的明长城使用了特殊的石灰砂浆，使墙体更加坚固。

paragraphs.en: The Wall served not only as a defense but also as a communication system. Beacon towers were built at intervals, where soldiers would light fires to warn of approaching enemies. This allowed messages to travel over hundreds of kilometers in just a few hours.
paragraphs.zh: 长城不仅用于防御，还作为一个通信系统。烽火台按一定间距建造，士兵们点火警示敌人接近。这使得消息在短短几小时内就能传遍数百公里。

paragraphs.en: Building the Wall was an enormous engineering challenge. Workers had to carry materials up steep mountains and across rivers. Many sacrificed their lives, which led to the Wall being called 'the longest cemetery on Earth.' However, recent studies suggest the actual death toll was lower than legends claim.
paragraphs.zh: 建造长城是一个巨大的工程挑战。工人们必须将材料运上陡峭的山峰，跨过河流。许多人牺牲了生命，这使得长城被称为“地球上最长的墓地”。然而，最近的研究表明实际死亡人数低于传说中所述。

paragraphs.en: Over time, the Wall's military significance declined. With the expansion of China's borders and the invention of more advanced weapons, the Wall no longer served as a primary defense. By the Qing Dynasty (1644–1912), it became more of a symbol of Chinese civilization than a practical military structure.
paragraphs.zh: 随着时间的推移，长城的军事意义有所下降。随着中国疆域的扩展和更先进武器的发明，长城不再作为主要防御工事。到了清朝（1644–1912年），它更多成为中华文明的象征，而非实用的军事结构。

paragraphs.en: Today, the Great Wall is a UNESCO World Heritage site and a major tourist attraction. Millions of visitors walk its restored sections each year. However, many parts are in ruin due to natural erosion and human damage. Conservation efforts are ongoing to preserve this ancient wonder for future generations.
paragraphs.zh: 今天，长城是联合国教科文组织世界遗产和主要旅游景点。每年有数百万游客参观其修复的段落。然而，由于自然侵蚀和人为破坏，许多部分已成废墟。保护工作正在进行，以为后代保存这一古代奇迹。

paragraphs.en: The Great Wall represents the resilience and creativity of the Chinese people. It is not just a wall; it is a monument to human effort and determination. As the saying goes, 'He who has not climbed the Great Wall is not a true man.'
paragraphs.zh: 长城象征着中国人民的韧性和创造力。它不仅仅是一道墙，更是人类努力和决心的纪念碑。俗话说：“不到长城非好汉”。

questions.q: What is the main purpose of the first unified Great Wall under Qin Shi Huang?
options: To connect existing walls for defense against northern invaders
To create a trade route between east and west
To serve as a symbol of wealth
To mark the boundary of the empire
questions.explain: 文章第二段明确指出，秦始皇下令连接已有的城墙，形成连续的防御线以抵御北方入侵者。因此正确答案为A。

questions.q: Which material was commonly used in the Ming Dynasty sections of the Great Wall?
questions.explain: 文章第三段提到，明朝时期砖块和灰浆变得普遍，因此正确答案为C。

questions.q: What does the word 'beacon' in paragraph 4 most likely mean?
options: A signal fire on a tower
questions.explain: 根据上下文，烽火台用于点火警示敌人接近，因此'beacon'在此指塔上的信号火，正确答案为B。

questions.q: Why did the military significance of the Great Wall decline?
options: Because the wall collapsed in many places
Because China's borders expanded and weapons advanced
Because the Qing Dynasty destroyed parts of it
questions.explain: 文章第六段提到，随着中国疆域扩展和更先进武器的发明，长城不再作为主要防御工事，因此正确答案为B。
